chunk_summary: Vitamin A prophylaxis and deficiency management are critical in pediatric nutrition, focusing on prevention, therapeutic dosing for deficiency, and integration with malnutrition care protocols in India.
learning_objectives: Understand the role of vitamin A prophylaxis in preventing xerophthalmia and reducing morbidity risk.
Learn therapeutic dosing protocols for vitamin A deficiency in children with severe acute malnutrition (SAM).
Recognize the importance of integrating vitamin A actions with immunization and malnutrition care programs.
key_facts: Vitamin A prophylaxis schedule in India: 100,000 IU at 9 months, 200,000 IU at 16–18 months, and every 6 months up to 5 years.
Therapeutic dosing for SAM: <6 months 50,000 IU, 6–12 months (or <8 kg) 100,000 IU, >12 months 200,000 IU.
Deficiency management includes repeat dosing on days 1, 2, and 14 for clinical signs like night blindness or Bitot spots.
Missed prophylaxis doses should prompt linkage to immunization or VHSND sessions.
Avoid duplicate high-dose administration within one month unless deficiency is evident.
reference_quote: Operational prophylaxis in India is commonly delivered as a high-dose schedule linked to immunization contacts: 100,000 IU at 9 months, then 200,000 IU at 16–18 months, followed by 200,000 IU every 6 months up to 5 years depending on local program implementation.
document_key_topics: Vitamin A prophylaxis schedule and implementation
Deficiency management protocols for severe acute malnutrition
Integration of vitamin A actions with immunization and malnutrition care
